vulnerabilities.title: Error-Based SQL Injection in 'id' Parameter
vulnerabilities.description: A SQL injection vulnerability was detected using Error-Based technique on parameter 'id'. The MySQL backend processes unsanitized user input, allowing an attacker to read, modify, or delete database contents.
vulnerabilities.evidence: Database error signature detected: 'you have an error in your sql syntax' — MySQL 8.0 error exposed
vulnerabilities.remediation:
1. Use parameterized queries or prepared statements.
2. Implement input validation with allowlists.
3. Apply least privilege on DB accounts.
4. Enable WAF with SQL injection rules.

vulnerabilities.title: Default Credentials Accepted — admin:admin
vulnerabilities.description: The application accepts the default credentials admin:admin on the administrative login endpoint. An attacker can immediately gain full administrative access.
vulnerabilities.evidence: HTTP 302 redirect to /admin/dashboard received after posting default credentials
vulnerabilities.remediation:
1. Remove or change all default credentials immediately.
2. Enforce strong password policy (min 12 chars, mixed case, numbers, symbols).
3. Implement MFA on admin accounts.
4. Add account lockout after 5 failed attempts.

vulnerabilities.title: Time-Based Blind SQL Injection in 'order' Parameter
vulnerabilities.description: Time-based blind SQL injection detected in the 'order' parameter. The server delayed response by 3.2 seconds after injecting SLEEP(3), indicating vulnerable query construction.
vulnerabilities.evidence: Response delayed by 3.2s (threshold=2.5s) after sleep payload injection
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use parameterized queries. Validate sort parameters against an allowlist of permitted values.

vulnerabilities.title: Reflected XSS in Search Parameter 'q'
vulnerabilities.description: XSS payload reflected unencoded in HTTP response. Parameter 'q' is vulnerable to reflected cross-site scripting.
vulnerabilities.evidence: Payload '<script>alert(1)</script>' reflected verbatim in HTML response at position 847
vulnerabilities.remediation:
1. Apply context-aware output encoding.
2. Implement Content Security Policy (CSP).
3. Use auto-escaping template engines.

vulnerabilities.title: Horizontal Privilege Escalation via User ID Enumeration
vulnerabilities.description: The /users/{id}/profile endpoint allows access to any user's profile by changing the numeric ID parameter. No authorization check verifies resource ownership.
vulnerabilities.evidence: HTTP 200 received for user_id=1235 while authenticated as user_id=1234
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement object-level authorization. Verify that the current session user owns the requested resource on every request.

vulnerabilities.title: Unauthenticated Access to Admin API Endpoint
vulnerabilities.description: /api/v1/admin/users returns a full user list without requiring authentication. Any unauthenticated visitor can enumerate all user accounts.
vulnerabilities.evidence: HTTP 200 returned with JSON array of 847 user objects (no auth required)
vulnerabilities.remediation: Require authentication and authorization (admin role) on all /admin/* API routes. Remove debug endpoints in production.

vulnerabilities.title: Session Cookie Missing Security Attributes
vulnerabilities.description: The session cookie is set without HttpOnly, Secure, or SameSite attributes, making it accessible to JavaScript and transmittable over HTTP.
vulnerabilities.evidence: Set-Cookie: session=abc123; Path=/ (missing HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite)
vulnerabilities.remediation: Set-Cookie: session=...; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict; Path=/

vulnerabilities.title: DOM-Based XSS in Comment Field
vulnerabilities.description: The comment input field in /posts/{id}/comments writes user input directly to the DOM via innerHTML, enabling DOM-based XSS.
vulnerabilities.evidence: Payload '' reflected in DOM response without encoding
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use textContent instead of innerHTML. Sanitize with DOMPurify. Implement strict CSP.
